_comment: Brain-root hooks. Loaded for any session opened at brain/ or below. The gielinor/.claude/settings.json holds architectural guarantees scoped to gielinor sessions; this file holds cross-brain instrumentation.
_comment_abs_path: S052: hook commands use an absolute path instead of ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}. Sessions launched from a subdirectory (e.g. switchboard/, experiments/) come up with an empty CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, which expanded to a broken '/developer-braindead/...' path and silently no-op'd every hook — that session never appeared on the switchboard or emitted events. Absolute path is machine-specific but the brain hasn't migrated hosts; revisit at ascension (death-as-migration).
_comment_status_sidecar: D-020 status-sidecar.py registered for UserPromptSubmit + Stop + SessionEnd (3 fires/turn). PreToolUse/PostToolUse NOT registered broadly — per-tool granularity isn't load-bearing and would push fire rate to 2N+3/turn. EXCEPTION (S053): status-sidecar IS registered for PreToolUse/PostToolUse matched ONLY to AskUserQuestion|ExitPlanMode — these park the session on the user mid-turn with no Stop, so the board would read WORKING while actually WAITING. Tight matcher keeps it ~0-2 fires/session. S057 EXTENDED the same matcher to Task|Agent for the waiting_for_subagents (AWAITING CREW) state — a foreground spawn blocks the parent until the crew returns; Pre sets the state, Post re-derives it. The manifest per-row override in _write_manifest is the authority (refreshes from any session's fire, self-heals a missed Post); the Pre/Post registration just covers the lone-blocked-session case. See S032 incident + S033 re-enable decision; lorebook draft on hook-fire-rate-as-budget pending.
_comment_session_start: status-sidecar.py on SessionStart carries a session's /rename board label across a /clear: /clear keeps claude.exe alive but mints a new session UUID (new sid8), orphaning the state-names.json label keyed on the old sid8. The hook acts only on source=='clear', matching the just-ended predecessor by shared claude.exe pid. Fires once per session — negligible cost.
_comment_grounding_cue: B-009 (lands the B-008 godly proposal; decision D-028): grounding-cue-reminder.py is the brain's first ADVISORY UserPromptSubmit hook (the six boundary hooks all BLOCK; this one only nudges via additionalContext, exit 0 always). On a continuation-cue prompt ('again', 'back to', 'the X I prepared earlier') it injects a 'check your own bank/notes/research first' reminder — the mechanism for the grounding-precondition that recurred 3x (S066/S076/S095) despite global-confirm + memory. Gielinor-scoped: skips dev-brain via the per-session status sidecar (actor=='braindead'). Verified at the hook boundary B-009 (cue→emit, no-cue→silent, braindead→skip, malformed→exit 0); live additionalContext injection follows the documented UserPromptSubmit contract. Tune _CUE_PATTERNS if noisy.
_comment_domain_cue: S-this (2026-06-02, dev): domain-cue-reminder.py is the generalized domain-knowledge-grounding hook — the registry-driven successor to shipping-cue-reminder.py (now retired). ADVISORY UserPromptSubmit (sibling of grounding-cue-reminder.py — nudges via additionalContext, exit 0 always, never blocks). It reads gielinor/.claude/hooks/cue_registry.py (the DOMAINS table) and, on any domain's cue match, injects 'load that domain's knowledge home, or spawn its specialist'. Entry #1 is shipping/mart, ported VERBATIM from S124's shipping-cue (cues: shipping/mart/carrier/shipment/parcel/freight/surcharge/tender/LPS/OML + carrier names; bare 'report'/'cost' EXCLUDED as wallpaper risk; nudge = 'load how_to.md §0 + reference, or spawn the shipping-agent'). Fixes the live failure where mart work was done as Jebrim-PRINCIPAL without loading shipping-agent/reference (the guarantee is baked into the SUB-AGENT config; the principal path had no trigger). The Nth domain (EU tender / FIF / SCM) is ONE ROW in cue_registry.py — no new hook file, no new settings line. Per-entry actor scope (skip_actors, default skips dev-brain/braindead via the per-session status sidecar); multiple matches emit ONE combined nudge (no stacking). DISTINCT from grounding-cue (that = identity reflex, continuation->own past work; this = domain reflex, topic->external knowledge home) — grounding-cue is untouched. Reinforced by the spawn-the-agent default in players/jebrim/CLAUDE.md + the calling-the-shipping-agent skill. Boundary-verified on synthetic payloads (cue->emit, carrier-name->emit, non-shipping->silent, bare-report->silent, braindead->skip, multi-domain->combined, malformed->exit 0); live-fire from a real player entry is the verify hand-off. Add a domain by appending one dict to cue_registry.py.
_comment_forced_read: S145 §X.4 (2026-06-02, dev): keepsake-forced-read.py is the ENFORCED pairing for the §X.10 `Reading:` line — the Reading: line only NAMES the grounding read at the Plan line (R3, visible-not-enforced); this hook FORCES it by injecting the keepsake CONTENTS into context (a guarantee, not a name). Converts the respawn keepsake-read from ritual DISCIPLINE (drifts — regression S145 case 7: a scout's 'keepsake empty' echoed without opening the full file) into a hook GUARANTEE. ADVISORY (additionalContext, exit 0 always, never blocks — sibling of grounding-cue/domain-cue). TWO ARMS: SessionStart inlines the GLOBAL keepsake (player-agnostic, known now) + a forcing directive to read the active player's keepsake + relevant inventory resume; the first UserPromptSubmit (actor resolvable then, via the hardened _actor.resolve_actor — the address arrives after SessionStart) inlines the ACTIVE player's keepsake/current.md ONCE (sentinel ~/.claude/status/<sid8>.fread). Scope: jebrim/zezima/guthix bear a keepsake; unscoped/wisp get only the global (no sentinel set, so a later player-address still injects); braindead skipped (no keepsake, respawn.md governs). Sub-agent SessionStart/prompt (agent_type set) skipped. The +39% memory-forcing analog. Pairs with domain-cue (topic->knowledge home) + grounding-cue (continuation->own past work). Fill keepsake/current.md to make the forced read worth obeying = principal/alching work, not this hook's. Boundary-verified on synthetic payloads (S-this). Registered on SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit at brain root (the default + cockpit launch dir).
_comment_draft_gate: S145 §X.3 (2026-06-02, dev): draft-gate-rewrite.py turns the two GUIDED-ONLY draft gates (write-rules.md L62-63: bank/notes/ + spellbook/skills/) into GUARANTEES — the sibling move to §X.4's forced-read. PreToolUse on Edit|Write|NotebookEdit|MultiEdit: when the MAIN agent writes a gielinor bank/notes/ or spellbook/skills/ path, it returns updatedInput (permissionDecision 'allow', full tool_input copied, only the path swapped) redirecting into bank/drafts/notes/ or spellbook/drafts/skills/ — the write just LANDS in the draft home instead of skipping the gate. Redirect-not-block (better ergonomics, can't be argued past); idempotent (a path already under drafts/ is left alone); exits 0 always (a bug falls through to a plain allow). EXEMPTIONS so promotion isn't broken: sub-agents (agent_type set — gnomes RUN alching, their bank/notes/ writes ARE promotions + they carry their own boundary hooks); braindead (D-032 full access, via _actor.resolve_actor); any active ritual .mode marker (alching/drafts/bankstanding/closing/consultation/wrapped_up — a canonical write during a tending ritual is a promotion). Failure calculus: exempt-too-little breaks a live ritual (high blast radius) > exempt-too-much == today's guided-only status quo (harmless), so exemptions bias wide and every redirect logs draft-gate:redirect for observability. gielinor-scoped (developer-braindead/ has no drafts gate). Boundary-verified on synthetic payloads (test_draft_gate_rewrite.py). Registered on the Edit matcher at brain root + gielinor/.claude/settings.json (defense-in-depth; idempotency makes double-fire safe). Pairs with §X.4 forced-read + the domain/grounding cues.
_comment_open_gate: 2026-05-28 (Khaan learnings item 1 — first POSITIVE enforcement gate; principal chose hard-block). require-open-on-entry.py blocks Edit|Write|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit of brain content in player + Braindead sessions until this sid8 has posted an OPEN to its comms/active.md — mechanizing the OPEN-on-entry discipline, the #1 historical leak (~70% of pre-S110 sessions). Escapes: the OPEN itself (comms/active.md), the dev-brain marker (.claude/active-mode.txt), intent sidecars (.claude/intent/*), narration; sub-agents (agent_type set); guthix/wisp/unknown actors; non-brain paths. Fails OPEN on any error (exit 0) like every other hook — a bug cannot brick a session. Actor via the per-session status sidecar (same source as grounding-cue-reminder.py). Verified at the boundary on 12 synthetic payloads (S-this; block fires for braindead+player no-OPEN, allow for OPEN-present/escapes/sub-agent/non-brain/malformed). Registered in BOTH settings files like the boundary hooks. LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-05-28 (D-033 Verification): hooks DO hot-reload mid-session; probe-instrumented run observed the allow path (braindead+OPEN -> exit 0, write succeeded) and the block path (actor flipped to jebrim, no OPEN -> exit-2 banner, file never created) through the real pipeline; probe then stripped.
_comment_boundary_hooks: S085 (Codex finding): the six architectural guarantees were wired ONLY in gielinor/.claude/settings.json via ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}. A session launched at brain root (the default per CLAUDE.md, AND what the cockpit does — ptybridge spawns claude with cwd=brain root) loaded only this root file's observability hooks, so the guarantees were prompt discipline, not gates (proven: an rm at brain root was NOT blocked). The boundary hooks now register HERE with absolute paths to gielinor/.claude/hooks/ so they fire repo-wide regardless of launch dir. They remain in gielinor/.claude/settings.json too; the redundancy is harmless (blocking hooks are idempotent — both pass or the first blocks) and is defense-in-depth in case the loader's behavior differs by launch dir. block-deletes is path-agnostic (applies brain-wide, matching dev-brain's own never-destroy discipline); block-confirmed-writes is gielinor-scoped via its own BRAIN_ROOT; the role hooks are agent_type-gated, so they are inert for principal sessions.
